issues.title: Scaffolder update: stop false-positive drift when a file matches the incoming template
issues.description: `to-execution update` classifies an invariant file as drifted whenever its current hash differs from the recorded stamped-form hash, even when the file is byte-identical to the incoming template (src/bin/cli.js:658 checks current != recorded before comparing current to templateHash). The stale anchor is then carried forward (cli.js:663), so the false positive repeats on every future update. Dogfood test: 8 of 9 reported-drifted files were identical to the template. Fix: when currentHash === templateHash, classify the file unchanged and record templateHash, before the drift check. End-to-end: run update on an Instance whose file lockstep-matches the new template — it reports unchanged, and the rewritten marker anchors the template hash.
acceptance_criteria: A file byte-identical to the incoming template is reported unchanged, never drifted, regardless of the recorded hash
After update, the version marker records the template hash for such files (stale anchor not carried forward)
A file that genuinely differs from both the recorded hash and the template is still reported drifted and left in place
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS on the change
Dogfood root re-run of update reports the 8 previously false-positive files as unchanged
issues.root_cause: update() orders the recorded-hash drift check before the template-content comparison, so lockstep-updated files with a stale anchor are misclassified
issues.fix: update() reordered to test currentHash === templateHash before the recorded-hash drift check: a file byte-identical to the incoming template is classified unchanged and re-anchored to the template hash. Commit 93f1489 (src/bin/cli.js). code-standards + package-qa PASS.
issues.notes: Found dogfood-testing the upgrade path (2026-06-10). The one genuine drift in that test (_teammate.template.md missing the color line) is being caught up directly.

issues.fix: Template ships .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ via .gitkeep on every init; stamp-policy adds both to WORK_TRACKING_DIR_PREFIXES so update classes them work-tracking and never touches records inside. Commit 7e4a7d8. code-standards + package-qa PASS.
issues.title: Two homes stamped: Template ships .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/, stamp policy classes them work-tracking
issues.description: The Template ships the two Progress Record / Runtime Record homes of ADR-0008: .excn/progress/ (agent- and gate-written) and .excn/runtime/ (hook-written), present on every fresh init. The stamp policy classifies both as work-tracking so update never touches their contents. The stamped .excn gitignore continues to match *_progress.json at any depth with no change. End-to-end: init a temp dir, both homes exist, update leaves files placed in them untouched.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh init stamps .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/
stamp policy classifies both directories as work-tracking (update never refreshes or drift-reports their contents)
Existing *_progress.json gitignore line covers records inside both homes with no gitignore edit
Src-First: lands in src/ first, dogfood root mirrors
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS

issues.title: Runtime Records move: hooks, doctor, and viewer-server read and write .excn/runtime/
issues.description: Hook-written state becomes Runtime Records: the invocation heartbeat ledger, the viewer-server pid record, and gate-watch state are written to and read from .excn/runtime/. doctor heartbeat checks and the viewer-server daemon read the new home. End-to-end: a hook fires, its invocation lands in .excn/runtime/hook-invocations_progress.json, doctor reports the heartbeat from there.
acceptance_criteria: All hook scripts write their Runtime Records under .excn/runtime/
doctor reads heartbeats and viewer-server state from .excn/runtime/ and reports feature health correctly
No hook writes any *_progress.json at the .excn base
Src-First: template hook sources first, dogfood root mirrors
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS

issues.title: Verdict Ledger schema: gates' append shape validates strictly
issues.description: A new verdict-ledger schema defines the append-only array of gate verdicts (step, at, artifact, summary). The session Verdict Ledger and a sprint record's step_log entries share the entry shape; the sprint schema references it rather than duplicating it. progress.schema.json remains the unit-of-work tracker shape. End-to-end: the live session ledger and sprint-6 step_log validate strictly against the new schema.
acceptance_criteria: verdict-ledger.schema.json ships in the Template's schemas
session_progress.json validates against it as-is
sprint.schema.json step_log entries reference the same entry shape (no duplicated definition)
progress.schema.json unchanged for trackers
Src-First: template schemas first, dogfood root mirrors

issues.title: Migrate command relocates Progress and Runtime Records; doctor flags the legacy flat layout
issues.description: A new Scaffolder migrate command relocates known records on an already-stamped Instance into their ADR-0008 homes: versioned migration table, idempotent re-run, location-only (content byte-identical), every move reported. doctor detects the legacy flat layout and names the command. update's never-touch-work-tracking contract is unchanged. End-to-end: a temp Instance with the legacy flat layout migrates cleanly; re-run is a no-op; the dogfood root is migrated as proof.
acceptance_criteria: migrate relocates known *_progress.json records into .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ by writer class
Re-running migrate is a no-op; file content is byte-identical after relocation
doctor reports the legacy flat layout and names migrate; reports clean after migration
update behavior unchanged (never touches work-tracking)
package-qa rubric extended to exercise the migrate contract (chartered change, alignment-gated per ADR-0004)
Dogfood root migrated: no *_progress.json remains at .excn base; hooks and viewer keep working
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS

issues.title: Progress-location deny guard: misfiled Progress Record writes are denied with a redirect
issues.description: A PreToolUse guard in the spawn-guard pattern denies a *_progress.json write outside .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/, with a redirect reason naming the right home. Rules/decision split, hooks.config toggle, every firing invocation-logged, fail-safe on any error (a broken guard never blocks legitimate writes). Location only — schema and content stay the gates' job. End-to-end: a simulated misfiled write is denied with the redirect; a correct-home write passes untouched.
acceptance_criteria: Misfiled *_progress.json write denied with a reason naming .excn/progress/ or .excn/runtime/
Writes inside the two homes pass untouched; non-progress writes unaffected
Toggle in hooks.config.json; malformed config or payload fails safe (exit 0, no output)
Every firing logs one invocation record
Payload-simulation tests cover deny, pass, and fail-safe paths
Src-First: template hook source first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes

issues.title: Viewer sprint switcher: past sprints readable from the status page
issues.description: The viewer gains a sprint switcher over the sprint files it already probes: the active sprint renders by default; selecting a closed sprint renders the same lanes plus its decisions, retrospective notes, and step_log gate verdicts. End-to-end: sprints 1 through 6 browsable on the live status page.
acceptance_criteria: Switcher lists every probed sprint, newest first, active sprint default
A closed sprint shows lanes, decisions, retrospective notes, and step_log verdicts
No server change; viewer stays read-only over .excn JSON
Src-First: template viewer first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes; verified on the live viewer server

issues.title: Viewer hook health: doctor-parity cards plus the invocation log
issues.description: The viewer renders per-feature hook health cards mirroring doctor (firing / stale / disabled, last heartbeat) from the Runtime Record invocation ledger, with a collapsible recent-invocations table filterable by feature and outcome. End-to-end: a stale or broken hook is visible on the status page at a glance, with the log entries to diagnose it.
acceptance_criteria: Per-feature cards show doctor-parity status and last heartbeat from .excn/runtime/hook-invocations_progress.json
Collapsible table shows recent invocations, filterable by feature and outcome
Missing or empty ledger renders an explicit empty state, not an error
Src-First: template viewer first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes; verified on the live viewer server
